Subject: Third Dispute  Unauthorized Macys Charge and Incorrect Account Balance


To Whom It May Concern,

This is my third dispute regarding the unauthorized charge of XXXX dated XXXX XXXX XXXX on my Macys/Citibank account. I previously reported this transaction and was under the impression that Macys Credit Card Services and Citibank were investigating the matter. However, I never received any temporary or permanent credit, nor did I receive any clear explanation regarding the outcome of the investigation.

Because this unauthorized charge was never properly resolved, the balance on my account became incorrect. I do not understand why this account was closed when I did not owe this debt. In fact, my last payment was XXXX, which exceeded the disputed amount, and based on my records, Macys actually owes me money.

I am requesting the following:

A full reinvestigation of the unauthorized transaction.
Written documentation of the prior investigation results.
Correction of the account balance.
Removal of any negative reporting associated with this disputed amount.
Reimbursement or credit for any overpayment made on the account.

XXXX. Failure to Conduct a Reasonable Investigation
Under FCRA 1681s-2(b), furnishers must conduct a meaningful investigation.
In Johnson v. MBNA America Bank, the court held that merely confirming internal 
records is insufficient.
In Cushman v. Trans Union Corp., the court required a genuine reinvestigation beyond
superficial verification.


Please review this matter immediately and provide a written response as soon as possible.

Sincerely,
ricardo pimienta
Account Number XXXX
